title: Semiclassical Ehrenfest paths in open quantum systems
abstract: We study the semiclassical Ehrenfest trajectories in open quantum systems. We first derive in explicit form the Fokker-Planck equation that governs the time evolution of the mixing measure for a Gaussian mixture. Then, we embed the generalized Ehrenfest theorem recently obtained for open quantum systems into this phase-space picture to study the time evolution of the expectations of observable with respect to the Gaussian mixture. We show how the coherent and irreversible contributions are microscopically separated. Our work provides a transparent phase-space interpretation of the emergence of classical trajectories in open quantum dynamics.
summary: This paper develops a phase-space framework to describe the semiclassical evolution of open quantum systems using Gaussian mixtures. It successfully decomposes the dynamics of expectation values into reversible, coherent parts and irreversible, diffusive parts. This provides a transparent way to see how classical trajectories emerge from quantum dynamics subject to environmental noise.
main_problem: Extending semiclassical Ehrenfest trajectories to open quantum systems to understand how classical trajectories emerge from dynamics involving dissipation and decoherence.
main_result: The derivation of a Fokker-Planck equation for a Gaussian mixture that provides a phase-space interpretation of the generalized Ehrenfest theorem, separating dynamics into coherent (unitary) and irreversible (diffusive) components.
method: Uses a Gaussian mixture framework in an extended phase space, employing a Lagrangian description and a local harmonic approximation to derive a Fokker-Planck equation and its equivalent Itô stochastic differential equation.
model_or_system: Open quantum systems described by a Lindblad master equation, specifically validated using a 1D free particle with a quadratic Hamiltonian and linear Lindblad operators.
key_observables: Expectation values of observables, position and momentum centroids, covariance matrices, and position variance.
important_parameters: Coupling strength (lambda), mass (m), Planck's constant (hbar), and the 'not-too-squeezed' condition parameter (xi).
main_assumptions: Relies on the local harmonic approximation (truncating Weyl symbols to second order) and requires that Gaussian states do not become too squeezed.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the evolution of position variance over time, comparing the analytical quantum solution, a quantum case with diffusion, and a classical comparison without environmental noise.
paper_structure: The paper begins by deriving a Fokker-Planck equation for the mixing measure of a Gaussian mixture, then embeds the generalized Ehrenfest theorem into this phase-space picture, and concludes with a numerical validation using a free-particle model to demonstrate the split between coherent and diffusive dynamics.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous microscopic link between quantum master equations and classical-like phase-space transport, offering a clear thermodynamic decomposition of work and heat in the context of decoherence.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The position variance ⟨ˆx2⟩(t) = x(t)2 + σxx(t) for three cases: The quantum case with Dab = diag(2ℏλ, 0) (blue dotted line); the classical case with D = 0 (grey dotted line); the analytical quantum solution (red line). The choices of parameters are specified in the main text.
